enonce.texte: Match the following words related to health and the body with their correct definitions. Write the letter of the definition next to the word.

questions.texte: Match each term to its definition.

sousQuestions.reponse: e. Organs in the chest that help you breathe
f. An illness that affects a person, animal, or plant
a. Pain or irritation in the pharynx, often when swallowing
g. A serious viral disease that weakens the immune system
c. A bacterial infection that mainly affects the lungs
b. A chronic disease characterized by compulsive drug seeking
d. The act of inhaling and exhaling tobacco smoke

enonce.texte: Read the descriptions of people's situations. Identify the health problem or risky behavior described from the vocabulary list: fever, headache, HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, smoking, alcohol, drug addiction.

questions.texte: Marc has a high body temperature (39°C), feels very hot, and is shivering. What does he probably have?
Jeanne cannot stop thinking about beer. Every day, she drinks large amounts and neglects her work. What is this a sign of?
Pierre has a persistent cough, night sweats, weight loss, and has been coughing up blood for two months. What disease is suspected?
Marie-Claire shares a contaminated needle with friends to inject heroin. What life-threatening virus is she at high risk of contracting?

intitule: Using Present Simple to Describe Health Habits

enonce.texte: Complete the sentences using the Present Simple form of the verbs in brackets. Then, classify the habits as 'Good for health' or 'Bad for health'.

questions.texte: A balanced diet (to help) ______ your body stay strong.
Smoking (to damage) ______ your lungs and (to cause) ______ cancer.
Doctors (to recommend) ______ drinking at least 2 liters of water every day.
Drug addiction (to destroy) ______ families and (to lead) ______ to crime.

enonce.texte: Read the letter from a teenager to an advice column. Then, write a reply giving advice. Use should / shouldn't and must / mustn't at least three times each.

questions.reponse: Example answer: Dear Paul, You should talk to a school counselor. You must not skip meals because your body needs energy. You should avoid friends who pressure you to smoke. You must remember that your health is more important than fitting in. You shouldn't be afraid to say 'no'.

sousQuestions.texte: Original letter: 'Dear Doctor, I am 14 years old. My friends smoke cigarettes and drink alcohol. They laugh at me because I refuse. I feel very alone. I also have headaches often and I don't sleep well. What should I do? — Paul, Douala'

intitule: Listening Comprehension: A Visit to the Clinic

enonce.texte:
Your teacher will read a dialogue between a doctor and a patient. Listen carefully and answer the questions below.

Transcript (for teacher):
Doctor: Good morning. What brings you here today?
Patient: Good morning, Doctor. I have a terrible headache and a sore throat. I also feel very hot.
Doctor: Let me check your temperature. You have a fever of 38.5°C. Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
Patient: No, but my father smokes at home.
Doctor: That can affect your lungs. You must rest, drink warm tea with honey, and avoid smoke. Here is a prescription for paracetamol.
Patient: Thank you, Doctor.

questions.texte: What two symptoms does the patient complain about?
According to the doctor, what can affect the patient's lungs?
questions.reponse: Smoke from the father (passive smoking / second-hand smoke)

questions.texte: List two pieces of advice the doctor gives.
questions.reponse: 1. Rest. 2. Drink warm tea with honey. 3. Avoid smoke.

enonce.texte:
You are a health officer in your community. Design a short poster (4-6 sentences) to warn young people about the dangers of one of these: smoking, alcohol, or drug addiction.

Your poster must include:

  • A title (using an imperative, e.g., 'Stop Smoking Now!')
  • At least two facts using the Present Simple tense.
  • One recommendation using 'should not'.
  • One obligation using 'must'.
  • Three vocabulary words from Lesson 8 (e.g., lungs, health, disease).
images: A simple poster design showing a warning about smoking with a damaged lung illustration and bold red text.

questions.texte: Write the full text of your poster.
questions.reponse: Example: 'PROTECT YOUR LUNGS! Smoking damages your lungs and causes deadly diseases like cancer. It also harms the health of people around you. You should not start smoking, even as a test. You must say NO to tobacco. Choose life, not cigarettes!'
